sections.heading: CONTEXT OF THE PROPOSAL
sections.summary: Context for financial data access proposal to balance data flow and privacy in EU financial sector.
sections.source_quote: Context of the proposal: EU aims to balance data flow and privacy in financial sector, enabling consumers and firms to control financial data access while addressing current inefficiencies in data sharing.

sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS, SUBSIDIARITY AND PROPORTIONALITY
sections.summary: Legal basis for EU financial data regulation ensuring internal market functioning and proportionality.
sections.source_quote: Legal basis: EU legislation on financial data sharing ensures internal market functioning, harmonized rules for cross-border financial institutions, and proportionality in administrative burden.

sections.heading: RESULTS
sections.summary: Describes results of ex-post evaluations, stakeholder consultations, and impact assessments for financial data access framework under GDPR.
sections.source_quote: Results of ex-post evaluations, stakeholder consultations, and impact assessments for financial data access framework under GDPR.

sections.heading: BUDGETARY IMPLICATIONS
sections.summary: Details budgetary implications of implementing financial data access framework, noting no EU budget impact and limited costs for national authorities.
sections.source_quote: Implementation of this proposal would not have an impact on the general budget of the European Union.

sections.heading: OTHER ELEMENTS
sections.summary: Provides monitoring and evaluation mechanisms for regulatory actions and detailed explanation of financial data access framework.
sections.dtia_summary: Explains financial data access framework with customer data sharing mechanisms and permission dashboards.

requirements.text: Data holders must make available customer data upon request
Data users must use data only for agreed purposes
Data holders must provide permission dashboards for customers

sections.source_quote: Data holders must make available to customers the data within the scope of this Regulation based on a request.

sections.heading: This Regulation applies to the following categories of customer data on:
sections.summary: Regulation applies to specific financial customer data categories including mortgage credit agreements, loans, savings, investments, insurance products, pension rights, and creditworthiness assessments.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines financial customer data categories covered by the regulation for data transfer impact assessments.

requirements.text: This Regulation applies to the following categories of customer data on: (a) mortgage credit agreements, loans and accounts, except payment accounts as defined in the Payment Services Directive (EU) 2015/2366, including data on balance, conditions and transactions; (b) savings, investments in financial instruments, insurance-based investment products, crypto-assets, real estate and other related financial assets as well as the economic benefits derived from such assets; including data collected for the purposes of carrying out an assessment of suitability and appropriateness in accordance with Article 25 of Directive 2014/65/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council; (c) pension rights in occupational pension schemes, in accordance with Directive 2009/138/EC and Directive (EU) 2016/2341 of the European Parliament and of the Council; (d) pension rights on the provision of pan-European personal pension products, in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2019/1238; (e) non-life insurance products in accordance with Directive 2009/138/EC, with the exception of sickness and health insurance products; including data collected for the purposes of a demands and needs assessment in accordance with Article 20 of Directive (EU) 2016/97 of the European Parliament and of the Council; (f) data which forms part of a creditworthiness assessment of a firm which is collected as part of a loan application process or a request for a credit rating.

sections.source_quote: This Regulation applies to the following categories of customer data on: (a) mortgage credit agreements, loans and accounts, except payment accounts as defined in the Payment Services Directive (EU) 2015/2366, including data on balance, conditions and transactions; (b) savings, investments in financial instruments, insurance-based investment products, crypto-assets, real estate and other related financial assets as well as the economic benefits derived from such assets; including data collected for the purposes of carrying out an assessment of suitability and appropriateness in accordance with Article 25 of Directive 2014/65/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council; (c) pension rights in occupational pension schemes, in accordance with Directive 2009/138/EC and Directive (EU) 2.016/2341 of the European Parliament and of the Council; (d) pension rights on the provision of pan-European personal pension products, in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2019/1238; (e) non-life insurance products in accordance with Directive 2009/138/EC, with the exception of sickness and health insurance products; including data collected for the purposes of a demands and needs assessment in accordance with Article 20 of Directive (EU) 2016/97 of the European Parliament and of the Council; (f) data which forms part of a creditworthiness assessment of a firm which is collected as part of a loan application process or a request for a credit rating.

sections.heading: This Regulation applies to the following entities when acting as data holders or data
sections.summary: Regulation applies to specific financial entities when acting as data holders or data users.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines financial entities covered by the regulation for data transfer impact assessments.

requirements.text: This Regulation applies to the following entities when acting as data holders or data users: (a) credit institutions; (b) payment institutions, including account information service providers and payment institutions exempted pursuant to Directive (EU) 2015/2366; (c) electronic money institutions, including electronic money institutions exempted pursuant to Directive 2009/110/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council; (d) investment firms; (e) crypto-asset service providers; (f) issuers of asset-referenced tokens; (g) managers of alternative investment funds; (h) management companies of undertakings for collective investment in transferable securities; (i) insurance and reinsurance undertakings; (j) insurance intermediaries and ancillary insurance intermediaries; (k) institutions for occupational retirement provision; (l) credit rating agencies; (m) crowdfunding service providers; (n) PEPP providers; (o) financial information service providers.

sections.source_quote: This Regulation applies to the following entities when acting as data holders or data users: (a) credit institutions; (b) payment institutions, including account information service providers and payment institutions exempted pursuant to Directive (EU) 2015/2366; (c) electronic money institutions, including electronic money institutions exempted pursuant to Directive 2009/110/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council; (d) investment firms; (e) crypto-asset service providers; (f) issuers of asset-referenced tokens; (g) managers of alternative investment funds; (h) management companies of undertakings for collective investment in transferable securities; (i) insurance and reinsurance undertakings; (j) insurance intermediaries and ancillary insurance intermediaries; (k) institutions for occupational retirement provision; (l) credit rating agencies; (m) crowdfunding service providers; (n) PEPP providers; (o) financial information service providers.

sections.source_quote: Financial information service providers that do not have an establishment in the Union but that require access to financial data in the Union shall designate, in writing, a legal or natural person as their legal representative in one of the Member States from where the financial information service provider intends to access financial data.

sections.heading: Financial information service providers shall mandate their legal representatives to
sections.summary: Financial information service providers must mandate their legal representatives to act on their behalf with competent authorities.
sections.dtia_summary: Providers must mandate their legal representatives to act on their behalf with competent authorities.

requirements.text: Financial information service providers shall mandate their legal representatives to be addressed in addition to or instead of the financial information service provider by the competent authorities on all issues necessary for the receipt of, compliance with and enforcement of this Regulation

sections.source_quote: Financial information service providers shall mandate their legal representatives to be addressed in addition to or instead of the financial information service provider by the competent authorities on all issues necessary for the receipt of, compliance with and enforcement of this Regulation.

sections.heading: The designated legal representative may be held liable for non-compliance with
sections.summary: Designated legal representatives may be held liable for non-compliance with obligations.
sections.dtia_summary: Designated legal representatives may be held liable for non-compliance with obligations.

requirements.text: The designated legal representative may be held liable for non-compliance with obligations under this Regulation, without prejudice to the liability and legal actions that could be initiated against the financial information service provider

sections.source_quote: The designated legal representative may be held liable for non-compliance with obligations under this Regulation, without prejudice to the liability and legal actions that could be initiated against the financial information service provider.

sections.heading: Financial information service providers shall notify the name, address, the electronic
sections.summary: Financial information service providers must notify their legal representative's contact details to the competent authority.
sections.dtia_summary: Providers must notify their legal representative's contact details to the competent authority.

requirements.text: Financial information service providers shall notify the name, address, the electronic mail address and telephone number of their legal representative to the competent authority in the Member State where that legal representative resides or is established. They shall ensure that that information is up to date

sections.source_quote: Financial information service providers shall notify the name, address, the electronic mail address and telephone number of their legal representative to the competent authority in the Member State where that legal representative resides or is established. They shall ensure that that information is up to date.

sections.heading: The designation of a legal representative within the Union pursuant to paragraph 1
sections.summary: Designation of a legal representative within the Union does not constitute an establishment in the Union.
sections.dtia_summary: Designation of a legal representative within the Union does not constitute an establishment in the Union.

requirements.text: The designation of a legal representative within the Union pursuant to paragraph 1 shall not constitute an establishment in the Union

sections.source_quote: The designation of a legal representative within the Union pursuant to paragraph 1 shall not constitute an establishment in the Union.

sections.heading: The competent authority shall grant an authorisation if the information and evidence
sections.summary: EBA must maintain electronic central register of authorised providers, providers accessing data in other Member States, and financial data sharing schemes.
sections.dtia_summary: EBA must maintain electronic central register of authorised providers, providers accessing data in other Member States, and financial data sharing schemes.

requirements.text: EBA shall develop, operate and maintain electronic central register containing: (a) authorised financial information service providers; (b) financial information service providers that have notified their intention to access data in a Member State other than their home Member State; (c) financial data sharing schemes agreed between data holders and data users

sections.source_quote: EBA shall develop, operate and maintain an electronic central register which contains the following information: (a) the authorised financial information service providers. (b) the financial information service providers that have notified their intention to access data in a Member State other than their home Member State. (c) the financial data sharing schemes agreed between data holders and data users.

sections.heading: The competent authority shall authorise a third country financial information service
sections.summary: Authorisation for third country financial information service providers requires compliance with Articles 12, 16, designation of legal representative, cooperation arrangements, and non-listing in high-risk jurisdictions.
sections.dtia_summary: Third country providers must comply with Articles 12, 16; designate legal representative; ensure cooperation with third country authorities; and not be in high-risk jurisdictions.

requirements.text: third country financial information service provider has complied with all conditions laid down in Article 12 and 16
third country financial information service provider has designated a legal representative pursuant to Article 13
competent authority establishes cooperation arrangement with third country authorities for information exchange
third country is not listed as high-risk jurisdiction under Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1675

sections.source_quote: The competent authority shall authorise a third country financial information service provider provided that all the following conditions are met: (a) the third country financial information service provider has complied with all conditions laid down in Article 12 and 16; (b) the third country financial information service provider has designated a legal representative pursuant to Article 13; (c) where the third country financial information service provider is subject to supervision, the competent authority shall seek to put in place an appropriate cooperation arrangement with the relevant competent authority of the third country where the financial information service provider is established, to ensure an efficient exchange of information; (d) the third country where the financial information service provider is established is not listed as a non-cooperative jurisdiction for tax purposes under the relevant Union policy or as a high-risk third-country jurisdiction that presents deficiencies in accordance with Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1675.

sections.heading: The competent authority shall grant an authorisation only if, taking into account the
sections.summary: Authorisation requires robust governance arrangements for financial information service providers including organisational structure, risk management, and internal controls.
sections.dtia_summary: Financial information service providers must have robust governance arrangements including organisational structure, risk management, and internal controls.

requirements.text: financial information service provider has robust governance arrangements for its information service business

sections.source_quote: The competent authority shall grant an authorisation only if, taking into account the need to ensure the sound and prudent management of a financial information service provider, the financial information service provider has robust governance arrangements for its information service business.

sections.heading: The competent authority shall grant an authorisation only if the laws, regulations or
sections.summary: EBA must enter withdrawals of authorisation or termination of financial data sharing schemes in the register.

sections.heading: Within 3 months of receipt of an application or, if the application is incomplete, of
sections.summary: Competent authority must respond to authorisation applications within 3 months with reasons for refusal.

competent authority shall give reasons where it refuses an authorisation

sections.source_quote: Within 3 months of receipt of an application or, if the application is incomplete, of all of the information required for the decision, the competent authority shall inform the applicant whether the authorisation is granted or refused. The competent authority shall give reasons where it refuses an authorisation.

sections.heading: The competent authority may withdraw an authorisation issued to a financial
sections.summary: Competent authority may withdraw authorisation if provider fails to use it within 12 months, renounces it, ceases business for >6 months, uses false statements, no longer meets conditions, or poses risk to consumers/data.
sections.dtia_summary: Authorisation may be withdrawn if provider does not use it within 12 months, renounces it, ceases business for >6 months, uses false statements, no longer meets conditions, or poses risk to consumers/data.

requirements.text: provider does not make use of authorisation within 12 months, expressly renounces authorisation or has ceased to engage in business for more than 6 months
provider obtained authorisation through false statements or irregular means
provider no longer meets conditions for granting authorisation or fails to inform on major developments
provider would constitute risk to consumer protection and data security

sections.source_quote: The competent authority may withdraw an authorisation issued to a financial information service provider only if the provider: (a) does not make use of the authorisation within 12 months, expressly renounces the authorisation or has ceased to engage in business for more than 6 months; (b) has obtained the authorisation through false statements or any other irregular means; (c) no longer meets the conditions for granting the authorisation or fails to inform the competent authority on major developments in this respect; (d) would constitute a risk to consumer protection and the security of data.

sections.source_quote: Competent authorities that use settlement agreements or expedited enforcement procedures pursuant to Article 19 shall adapt the relevant administrative penalties and other administrative measures provided for in Article 20 to the case concerned to ensure the proportionality thereof, in particular by considering the circumstances listed in paragraph 1.

sections.heading: Competent authorities exchanging information with other competent authorities
sections.summary: Member States may set higher periodic penalty payment amounts than those specified in Article 21.
sections.dtia_summary: Member States can establish higher periodic penalty payments than the EUR 30,000 for natural persons and 3% of daily turnover for legal persons.

requirements.text: Member States may provide for higher amounts of periodic penalty payments than those laid down in paragraph 1, third subparagraph

sections.source_quote: Member States may provide for higher amounts of periodic penalty payments than those laid down in paragraph 1, third subparagraph.

sections.heading: The competent authority shall not transmit information shared by other competent
sections.summary: Member States must ensure competent authorities can impose specific administrative fines for legal persons, including up to EUR 500,000/year and 2% of annual turnover.
sections.dtia_summary: Member States must allow competent authorities to impose fines up to EUR 500,000/year for legal persons and 2% of their annual turnover, with adjustments for consolidated financial statements.

requirements.text: Competent authorities have power to impose maximum fines of up to EUR 50,000 per infringement and EUR 500,000 per year for legal persons
Competent authorities have power to impose fines of 2% of the legal person's annual turnover

sections.source_quote: Member States shall, in accordance with national law, ensure that competent authorities have the power to impose, in relation to the infringements referred to in paragraph 1 committed by legal persons, maximum administrative fines of: (a) up to EUR 50 000 per infringement and up to a total of EUR 500 000 per year, or, in the Member States whose official currency is not the euro, the corresponding value in the official currency of that Member State on ... [OP please insert the date of entry into force of this Regulation]; (b) 2% of the total annual turnover of the legal person according to the last available financial statements approved by the management body;

sections.heading: Where obligations under this Regulation concern the processing of personal data,
sections.summary: Competent authorities must cooperate with supervisory authorities for personal data processing.
sections.dtia_summary: Competent authorities must cooperate with supervisory authorities established under GDPR for personal data processing.

requirements.text: Where obligations under this Regulation concern processing of personal data, competent authorities shall cooperate with supervisory authorities established pursuant to Regulation (EU) 2016/679

sections.source_quote: Where obligations under this Regulation concern the processing of personal data, competent authorities shall cooperate with the supervisory authorities established pursuant to Regulation (EU) 2016/679.

sections.summary: Financial institutions and service providers can access Union customers' data held by data holders in the Union.
sections.dtia_summary: Financial institutions and service providers can access Union customers' data held by data holders in the Union, under freedom of establishment or service provision.

requirements.text: Financial information service providers and financial institutions shall be allowed to have access to the data listed in Article 2(1) of Union customers held by data holders established in the Union, pursuant to the freedom to provide services or the freedom of establishment

sections.source_quote: Financial information service providers and financial institutions shall be allowed to have access to the data listed in Article 2(1) of Union customers held by data holders established in the Union, pursuant to the freedom to provide services or the freedom of establishment.

sections.summary: Commission must submit report on financial data access conditions for account information service providers 4 years after entry into force.

sections.source_quote: Before adopting a delegated act, the Commission shall consult experts signated by each Member State in accordance with the principles laid down in the Interinstitutional Agreement of 13 April 2.016 on Better Law-Making.
